data.title: Write failing integration tests for design fidelity validation
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Write failing integration tests for the perceptual diff validation pipeline: Figma PNG export, Playwright screenshot capture, pixelmatch comparison, threshold gating, and graceful degradation.
**Why**: TDD — tests must fail (RED) before implementation to ensure implementation is driven by verifiable acceptance criteria.
**Scope**:
- IN: Tests for Figma image export (rate-limit handling, caching, resolution normalization), Playwright screenshot (viewport matching, stabilization), pixelmatch comparison (score computation, threshold gating), graceful degradation (Playwright absent, 429 response), advisory ticket creation on skip
- OUT: Implementation code — tests only

## Done Definitions

- Tests must be run and confirmed failing (RED) before any implementation story begins. The failing run result must be recorded in a story note: .claude/scripts/dso ticket comment <test-story-id> "RED confirmed: <test output summary>"
- When this story is complete, integration tests cover: Figma image export with lastModified caching and 429 handling, Playwright screenshot at configured viewport with stabilization, pixelmatch comparison producing 0-100 score, threshold gating (above→fidelity_verified, below→fidelity_gap), graceful degradation when Playwright absent or API returns 429

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
